name: Tris-HCl
storage_conditions: Room temperature or 4°C; autoclavable
preparation_notes: Adjust pH at room temperature; pH decreases ~0.03 units per °C increase. pH 7.4 at 25°C will be ~7.7 at 4°C. Use HCl to adjust pH of Tris base.
incompatible_with: EDTA at high concentrations can chelate essential metal cofactors
safety_notes: Low toxicity; mild irritant

name: PBS (Phosphate-Buffered Saline)
working_concentration: 1x (137 mM NaCl, 2.7 mM KCl, 10 mM Na2HPO4, 1.8 mM KH2PO4, pH 7.4)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; autoclavable; protect from contamination
preparation_notes: pH is relatively temperature-insensitive unlike Tris. Do not autoclave with calcium or magnesium salts as they will precipitate.
incompatible_with: divalent cations at high concentrations (precipitate as phosphate salts)
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: TAE Buffer (Tris-Acetate-EDTA)
working_concentration: 1x (40 mM Tris, 20 mM acetic acid, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; check for precipitates before use
preparation_notes: Lower buffering capacity than TBE; not recommended for extended runs. Better resolution of large DNA fragments (>2 kb). Can be reused but buffering capacity declines.
safety_notes: Low toxicity

name: TBE Buffer (Tris-Borate-EDTA)
working_concentration: 0.5x or 1x (89 mM Tris, 89 mM boric acid, 2 mM EDTA, pH 8.3)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; discard if crystals form
preparation_notes: Higher buffering capacity than TAE; preferred for long electrophoresis runs. Better for small DNA fragments (<1 kb) and polyacrylamide gels. Borate inhibits many enzymes, so DNA must be purified from gel before enzymatic reactions.
incompatible_with: downstream enzymatic reactions (borate inhibits ligase and other enzymes)
safety_notes: Boric acid is a reproductive toxin at high doses

name: TE Buffer (Tris-EDTA)
storage_conditions: Room temperature or 4°C; autoclave to sterilize
preparation_notes: EDTA chelates divalent cations and inhibits DNases. Use low-EDTA TE (10 mM Tris, 0.1 mM EDTA) for DNA intended for enzymatic reactions, as EDTA can inhibit enzymes requiring Mg2+.
incompatible_with: Mg2+-dependent enzymatic reactions at high EDTA concentrations
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: RIPA Buffer
working_concentration: 1x (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 150 mM NaCl, 1% NP-40, 0.5% sodium deoxycholate, 0.1% SDS)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; add protease and phosphatase inhibitors fresh before use
preparation_notes: Add protease inhibitor cocktail and PMSF immediately before use. SDS may precipitate at 4°C; warm briefly to redissolve. Strong lysis buffer that disrupts most protein-protein interactions.
shelf_life: 6 months without inhibitors; use within hours after adding inhibitors
safety_notes: Contains SDS and detergents; avoid ingestion and skin contact

name: Laemmli Sample Buffer (SDS-PAGE Loading Buffer)
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; aliquot to avoid freeze-thaw cycles
preparation_notes: Contains 62.5 mM Tris-HCl pH 6.8, 2% SDS, 25% glycerol, 0.01% bromophenol blue. Add reducing agent (5% beta-mercaptoethanol or 100 mM DTT) fresh before use. Heat samples at 95°C for 5 minutes to denature proteins.
safety_notes: Contains SDS; reducing agent is toxic (beta-mercaptoethanol) and pungent

name: 6x DNA Loading Dye
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light
preparation_notes: Typical composition: 30% glycerol, 0.25% bromophenol blue, 0.25% xylene cyanol, 10 mM EDTA. Bromophenol blue migrates at ~300 bp in 1% agarose; xylene cyanol at ~4 kb.
safety_notes: Non-toxic
common_uses: loading DNA samples onto agarose gels

name: Western Blot Transfer Buffer
working_concentration: 1x (25 mM Tris, 192 mM glycine, 20% methanol, pH 8.3)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; add methanol fresh before use
preparation_notes: Methanol aids protein binding to membrane but can cause gel shrinkage and reduce transfer of high-MW proteins. Reduce methanol to 10% or omit for proteins >100 kDa. Add 0.1% SDS for large proteins. Do not adjust pH with acid/base.
incompatible_with: PVDF membranes require pre-wetting in methanol before assembly
safety_notes: Contains methanol which is toxic; use in a well-ventilated area

name: Blocking Buffer (5% non-fat dry milk in TBST)
working_concentration: 5% w/v non-fat dry milk in TBST
storage_conditions: Prepare fresh or store at 4°C for up to 1 week
preparation_notes: Do NOT use milk-based blocking for phospho-specific antibodies as milk contains casein (a phosphoprotein) which causes high background. Use BSA (3-5%) for phospho-antibodies instead.
shelf_life: 1 week at 4°C; prepare fresh for best results
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: TBST (Tris-Buffered Saline with Tween-20)
working_concentration: 1x (20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.6, 150 mM NaCl, 0.1% Tween-20)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; add Tween-20 to 1x solution
preparation_notes: Use 0.05-0.1% Tween-20. Higher Tween-20 can strip weakly bound antibodies. Do not use with alkaline phosphatase-conjugated antibodies if Tween-20 concentration is too high.
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: SSC Buffer (Saline-Sodium Citrate)
stock_concentration: 20x (3 M NaCl, 300 mM sodium citrate, pH 7.0)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; autoclavable
preparation_notes: Higher SSC concentrations allow more mismatched hybridization (lower stringency). Lower SSC = higher stringency washes.
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: Formamide (deionized)
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C in aliquots; deionize with mixed-bed resin before use; protect from light
preparation_notes: Deionize before use as formamide degrades to formic acid and ammonium over time. Lowers the melting temperature of nucleic acid duplexes by ~0.72°C per 1% formamide. Must be molecular biology grade.
shelf_life: 6 months at -20°C (deionized); check pH before use
safety_notes: Teratogenic; reproductive toxin; avoid skin contact and inhalation; use in fume hood

name: MOPS Buffer
working_concentration: 1x (20 mM MOPS, 5 mM sodium acetate, 1 mM EDTA, pH 7.0)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light; discard if solution turns yellow
preparation_notes: Autoclaving can cause yellowing; filter-sterilize preferred. MOPS is light-sensitive. pH range 6.5-7.9. Used with formaldehyde for denaturing RNA gels.
safety_notes: Low toxicity; mild irritant

name: HEPES Buffer
storage_conditions: Room temperature or 4°C; filter-sterilize for cell culture
preparation_notes: pH relatively temperature-insensitive (dpH/dT = -0.014/°C). Provides buffering in pH range 6.8-8.2. Do not autoclave for cell culture use; filter-sterilize. Can generate cytotoxic reactive oxygen species when exposed to ambient light.
incompatible_with: prolonged light exposure in cell culture (generates ROS and hydrogen peroxide)
safety_notes: Low toxicity; avoid prolonged light exposure in cell culture media containing HEPES

name: Sodium Acetate (3 M, pH 5.2)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; autoclavable
preparation_notes: pH 5.2 is optimal for DNA and RNA precipitation with ethanol. Use pH 7.0 for precipitation of dNTPs and small oligonucleotides.
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: Ammonium Acetate (7.5 M or 10 M)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; filter-sterilize
preparation_notes: Does not co-precipitate dNTPs or salt as efficiently, leaving cleaner DNA. Isopropanol precipitation with ammonium acetate reduces salt carryover. Not suitable for RNA precipitation (inhibits T4 polynucleotide kinase and some other enzymes).
safety_notes: Low toxicity
common_uses: ethanol precipitation of DNA (removes free nucleotides)

name: Lithium Chloride (8 M)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; filter-sterilize or autoclave
preparation_notes: Selectively precipitates RNA; does not efficiently precipitate DNA, proteins, or carbohydrates. Preferred for RNA precipitation from in vitro transcription reactions.
safety_notes: Harmful if ingested in large quantities
common_uses: removal of free nucleotides from RNA samples

name: Glycerol
storage_conditions: Room temperature; autoclavable; hygroscopic
preparation_notes: Viscous; use positive-displacement pipettes or cut pipette tips for accurate measurement. 50% glycerol does not freeze at -20°C, making it useful for enzyme storage.
safety_notes: Non-toxic; GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe)

name: DMSO (Dimethyl Sulfoxide)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; hygroscopic; use molecular biology grade; freezing point 18.5°C
preparation_notes: Freezes at 18.5°C; warm to room temperature before opening. Cell culture grade DMSO must be used for cryopreservation. Enhances skin penetration of dissolved compounds.
safety_notes: Rapidly penetrates skin and carries dissolved substances through skin; wear appropriate gloves; low intrinsic toxicity but can deliver toxic solutes transdermally
common_uses: PCR additive to resolve GC-rich templates (2-10%)

name: Ethidium Bromide
working_concentration: 0.5 µg/mL in gel or staining solution
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light; amber bottle or foil-wrapped
preparation_notes: Intercalates between base pairs; fluorescence increases ~20-fold when bound to dsDNA. Can be added to gel before casting or used as a post-stain. Excitation 302 nm (UV), emission 590 nm (orange).
safety_notes: Potent mutagen and suspected carcinogen; wear nitrile gloves (latex is permeable); dispose of as hazardous waste; decontaminate with activated charcoal or commercial EtBr destaining bags

name: SYBR Green I
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; protect from light; DMSO stock does not freeze
preparation_notes: Binds dsDNA minor groove; fluorescence increases >1000-fold upon binding. More sensitive than ethidium bromide (~25-100x). Excitation 497 nm, emission 520 nm (green). Inhibits PCR at high concentrations.
safety_notes: Less mutagenic than ethidium bromide but still a DNA-binding compound; handle with gloves; dispose as hazardous waste

name: SYBR Safe
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; protect from light
preparation_notes: Designed as a safer alternative to ethidium bromide. Can be visualized with blue-light transilluminators (reduces UV-induced DNA damage) or UV. Excitation 280/502 nm, emission 530 nm.
safety_notes: Not classified as hazardous waste in many jurisdictions; negative in Ames test; still use gloves when handling
common_uses: safer alternative to ethidium bromide for teaching labs

name: DAPI (4',6-Diamidino-2-Phenylindole)
storage_conditions: Store stock at -20°C; protect from light; aliquot to avoid freeze-thaw
preparation_notes: Binds to AT-rich regions of minor groove of dsDNA. Cell-permeable for live or fixed cells. Excitation 360 nm, emission 460 nm (blue). Can also bind RNA with shifted emission (red).
safety_notes: Potential mutagen; avoid skin contact and inhalation; use gloves

name: Coomassie Brilliant Blue (R-250 and G-250)
stock_concentration: 0.1-0.25% in 50% methanol, 10% acetic acid (R-250); or as colloidal for G-250
storage_conditions: Room temperature; filter before use to remove particulates
preparation_notes: R-250: Fix and stain simultaneously in methanol/acetic acid. G-250: Used in colloidal (overnight, more sensitive) or Bradford assay format. Detection limit ~10-50 ng protein (gel), ~1 µg/mL (Bradford).
incompatible_with: detergents >0.1% SDS interfere with Bradford assay (G-250)
safety_notes: Stains skin and clothing; low toxicity

name: Ponceau S
storage_conditions: Room temperature
preparation_notes: Reversible stain; can be completely removed by washing with water or TBST. Stain membrane 1-5 minutes, destain with water. Detection limit ~250 ng protein per band. Used as loading control for western blots.
shelf_life: 12 months; can be reused multiple times
safety_notes: Low toxicity; stains skin temporarily
common_uses: reversible membrane protein staining after western blot transfer

name: Trypan Blue (0.4%)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; filter if precipitates form
preparation_notes: Exclusion dye: dead cells with compromised membranes stain blue; viable cells exclude the dye. Count within 3-5 minutes of staining as prolonged exposure kills cells. Use hemocytometer or automated cell counter.
incompatible_with: prolonged incubation times >5 min (increases false positives for dead cells)
safety_notes: Suspected carcinogen (azo dye); handle with gloves

name: Crystal Violet
storage_conditions: Room temperature; protect from light
preparation_notes: Stains DNA in nuclei of adherent cells. Can be solubilized with 10% acetic acid or methanol for quantification by absorbance at 570-590 nm.
safety_notes: Stains skin and clothing; potential irritant; suspected carcinogen with chronic exposure

name: Propidium Iodide (PI)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light
preparation_notes: Membrane-impermeant; only enters dead/permeabilized cells. Intercalates dsDNA and dsRNA (use RNase A to eliminate RNA signal for cell cycle analysis). Excitation 535 nm, emission 617 nm (red).
incompatible_with: live cell membrane-intact staining (must be used on fixed/permeabilized cells for intracellular staining)
safety_notes: Potential mutagen (DNA intercalator); handle with gloves

name: Acridine Orange
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light
preparation_notes: Differentially stains DNA (green fluorescence, 525 nm) and RNA (red/orange fluorescence, 650 nm). Cell-permeable; can be used on live cells. Metachromatic dye.
safety_notes: Potential mutagen; handle with gloves; protect from light
common_uses: cell viability with DAPI or ethidium bromide

name: Agarose
working_concentration: 0.5-2% w/v depending on DNA fragment size
storage_conditions: Room temperature; keep dry; tightly sealed
preparation_notes: 0.5% for large fragments (1-30 kb), 1% for 0.5-10 kb, 1.5% for 0.2-3 kb, 2% for 0.1-2 kb. Dissolve in TAE or TBE by microwaving. Low-melting point agarose available for gel extraction. Use molecular biology grade (low EEO) for best resolution.
safety_notes: Non-toxic; caution with hot molten agarose (superheating risk in microwave)
common_uses: agarose gel electrophoresis of DNA and RNA

name: Acrylamide/Bis-acrylamide (30%, 29:1)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light; use within expiration date
preparation_notes: Polymerizes when TEMED and APS are added. Higher percentage gels resolve smaller proteins. 29:1 ratio is standard for proteins; 19:1 for DNA sequencing gels. Degas solution before polymerization for best results.
safety_notes: NEUROTOXIN in unpolymerized form; cumulative neurotoxicity; always wear gloves; avoid inhalation of powder; polymerized acrylamide is non-toxic

name: SDS (Sodium Dodecyl Sulfate)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; precipitates below 15°C; store powder in dry conditions
preparation_notes: Anionic detergent that denatures proteins and confers uniform negative charge (~1.4 g SDS per g protein). Do not autoclave solutions. Stock precipitates at low temperatures; warm to dissolve. Use electrophoresis grade.
incompatible_with: potassium salts (forms insoluble potassium dodecyl sulfate precipitate)
safety_notes: Irritant to eyes, skin, and respiratory tract; wear dust mask when weighing powder

name: EDTA (Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; autoclavable
preparation_notes: Disodium EDTA will not dissolve until pH is adjusted to ~8.0 with NaOH. Chelates divalent cations (Mg2+, Ca2+, Mn2+, Zn2+). Inhibits metalloproteinases and DNases by chelating required metal cofactors.
incompatible_with: enzymes requiring divalent cation cofactors (restriction enzymes, polymerases, ligases need Mg2+)
safety_notes: Low toxicity; irritant
common_uses: component of TE, TAE, TBE buffers

name: DTT (Dithiothreitol)
storage_conditions: Store powder at 4°C desiccated; stock solutions at -20°C in aliquots
preparation_notes: Reduces disulfide bonds in proteins. Less pungent than beta-mercaptoethanol. Oxidizes in solution; prepare fresh or use frozen aliquots. Not compatible with maleimide or iodoacetamide-based labeling (until removed). pKa of thiol groups 9.2 and 10.1.
shelf_life: Powder: 12 months; 1 M stock at -20°C: 6 months
safety_notes: Irritant; harmful if inhaled or ingested; wear gloves

name: Beta-Mercaptoethanol (2-Mercaptoethanol, BME)
working_concentration: 5% (v/v) in Laemmli buffer; 1-15 mM in other applications
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C or room temperature; tightly sealed; keep in fume hood when open
preparation_notes: Strong reducing agent for disulfide bonds. Add fresh to sample buffers. 5% v/v = ~710 mM in Laemmli buffer. More volatile and pungent than DTT. 1 µL per 1 mL is typical for RNA work to inhibit RNases.
safety_notes: Toxic; strong unpleasant odor; use in fume hood; harmful by inhalation and skin absorption; causes eye and skin burns

name: TEMED (Tetramethylethylenediamine)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; tightly sealed; moisture-sensitive; nitrogen blanket recommended
preparation_notes: Catalyzes APS-initiated free radical polymerization of acrylamide. Add TEMED last when casting gels. More TEMED = faster polymerization. Old or oxidized TEMED results in poor polymerization.
shelf_life: 12 months; replace if gels fail to polymerize
incompatible_with: should be added last to gel mixture to control polymerization timing
safety_notes: Flammable; corrosive; toxic by inhalation; use in fume hood; strong amine odor
common_uses: polyacrylamide gel polymerization catalyst (SDS-PAGE, native PAGE, sequencing gels)

name: APS (Ammonium Persulfate)
storage_conditions: Powder at room temperature, desiccated. 10% solution at -20°C in aliquots; do not repeatedly freeze-thaw
preparation_notes: Free radical source for acrylamide polymerization with TEMED. 10% solution degrades over time; prepare fresh weekly or freeze aliquots. Old APS causes poor gel polymerization.
shelf_life: Powder: 24 months; 10% solution: 2 weeks at 4°C or 6 months at -20°C
safety_notes: Oxidizer; irritant; may cause allergic sensitization; wear gloves and mask when weighing
common_uses: initiator for polyacrylamide gel polymerization (with TEMED)

name: Phenol (Tris-saturated, pH 8.0)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light; must be equilibrated to pH >7.6 for DNA extraction; pH <7.0 for RNA
preparation_notes: pH 8.0 phenol: DNA partitions to aqueous phase. Acidic phenol (pH 4.5): DNA partitions to organic phase, RNA stays aqueous (basis of TRIzol). Always check pH before use as oxidation lowers pH. Pink/brown color indicates oxidation—discard.
incompatible_with: oxidized/discolored phenol causes nucleic acid damage
safety_notes: Highly toxic; causes severe chemical burns; rapidly absorbed through skin; use in fume hood; have PEG 300/400 available for skin decontamination (NOT water first)
common_uses: protein removal from nucleic acid preparations

name: Chloroform
storage_conditions: Room temperature; amber bottle; well-ventilated area
preparation_notes: Used as chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1) mixture. Removes residual phenol from aqueous phase. Denatures proteins at the interface. Isoamyl alcohol reduces foaming and helps separate phases.
safety_notes: Toxic by inhalation; suspected carcinogen; CNS depressant; use in fume hood; use polypropylene tubes only

name: Isoamyl Alcohol (3-Methyl-1-butanol)
working_concentration: 1 part in 24 parts chloroform (24:1 chloroform:isoamyl alcohol)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; flammable; keep away from heat/sparks
preparation_notes: Mixed with chloroform at 24:1 ratio. Reduces foaming during extraction and aids phase separation. Phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) is the standard extraction mixture.
safety_notes: Flammable; irritant; use in fume hood

name: Isopropanol (2-Propanol)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; flammable; molecular biology grade
preparation_notes: Use 0.6-0.7 volumes (vs 2-2.5 volumes for ethanol) for DNA precipitation. Precipitates at room temperature (no need for cold). Produces smaller, more compact pellets than ethanol. Coprecipitates more salt than ethanol—follow with 70% ethanol wash.
safety_notes: Flammable; CNS depressant at high concentrations; use in well-ventilated area

name: Ethanol (70% and 100%)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; flammable; keep tightly sealed (hygroscopic); use molecular biology grade for nucleic acid work
preparation_notes: Use 2-2.5 volumes 100% ethanol with salt for DNA/RNA precipitation. 70% ethanol wash removes residual salts from nucleic acid pellets. 70% ethanol is more effective as a disinfectant than 100% (water aids protein denaturation).
shelf_life: 24 months (keep sealed to prevent water absorption)
safety_notes: Highly flammable; keep away from heat/flame; use in ventilated area

name: Sodium Chloride (NaCl)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; hygroscopic; keep dry
preparation_notes: Essential for adjusting ionic strength. 0.9% NaCl = physiological saline (154 mM). Autoclavable. Higher NaCl increases stringency in hybridization washes.
safety_notes: Non-toxic at normal concentrations

name: Magnesium Chloride (MgCl2)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; very hygroscopic; keep tightly sealed
preparation_notes: Essential cofactor for DNA polymerases, restriction enzymes, and ligases. Excess Mg2+ decreases PCR specificity; too little reduces yield. Typical PCR range 1.5-3 mM. EDTA chelates Mg2+, so account for EDTA in calculations.
incompatible_with: phosphate buffers at high concentrations (forms precipitate)
safety_notes: Low toxicity; irritant at high concentrations

name: Calcium Chloride (CaCl2)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; very hygroscopic; anhydrous form absorbs water readily
preparation_notes: Used to make chemically competent E. coli cells. Autoclave or filter-sterilize solutions. Do not mix with phosphate buffers (precipitates calcium phosphate). CaCl2 treatment permeabilizes bacterial cell membranes to DNA uptake.
safety_notes: Irritant; exothermic when dissolving; handle with care

name: Potassium Chloride (KCl)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; keep dry
preparation_notes: Component of standard PCR buffers (50 mM) and physiological buffers. Promotes primer annealing to template DNA. High concentrations (>50 mM) can inhibit Taq polymerase.
safety_notes: Non-toxic at normal concentrations

name: TRIzol / TRI Reagent
working_concentration: 1 mL per 50-100 mg tissue or per 5-10 × 10^6 cells
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light
preparation_notes: Monophasic solution of phenol and guanidinium isothiocyanate. After chloroform addition and centrifugation: aqueous phase (RNA), interphase (DNA), organic phase (protein). RNA should be in the upper aqueous phase. Do NOT vortex after adding chloroform (shear RNA); mix by inversion.
incompatible_with: DEPC-treated water not required (guanidinium inactivates RNases)
safety_notes: Contains phenol and guanidinium thiocyanate; toxic and corrosive; use in fume hood; causes chemical burns; avoid skin/eye contact
common_uses: simultaneous extraction of RNA, DNA, and protein
RNA isolation from tissues and cells

name: Guanidinium Thiocyanate (Guanidine Thiocyanate)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; hygroscopic; protect from light
preparation_notes: Powerful chaotrope and protein denaturant. Inactivates RNases immediately—gold standard for RNA preservation. Component of TRIzol and most silica column RNA extraction kits. Solutions may need warming to dissolve.
safety_notes: Toxic if ingested; irritant; NEVER mix with bleach—produces lethal cyanide gas; use in fume hood

name: Formaldehyde (37% solution, formalin)
stock_concentration: 37% (w/v) aqueous solution, often with 10-15% methanol stabilizer
storage_conditions: Room temperature; keep tightly sealed; avoid temperatures below 15°C (polymerizes to paraformaldehyde at low temperatures)
preparation_notes: Fixes by cross-linking primary amines (lysine residues) in proteins. 10% neutral buffered formalin is the standard histology fixative. Methanol-free formaldehyde preferred for fluorescence applications. Fix time varies: cells 10-20 min, tissues 24-48 hr.
shelf_life: 12 months; discard if white precipitate (paraformaldehyde) forms
incompatible_with: do not mix with HCl (produces carcinogenic bis-chloromethyl ether)
safety_notes: Known human carcinogen (IARC Group 1); toxic by inhalation; sensitizer; use in fume hood; 0.75 ppm OSHA TWA exposure limit

name: Paraformaldehyde (PFA)
storage_conditions: Powder at room temperature; 4% solution at -20°C in aliquots or at 4°C for 1-2 weeks; 16% ampoules at room temperature until opened
preparation_notes: PFA is polymerized formaldehyde that depolymerizes to formaldehyde in solution. Dissolve powder by heating to 60°C in water with NaOH, then buffer with PBS. Methanol-free PFA preferred for immunofluorescence. Do not overheat (>68°C generates formic acid).
shelf_life: 4% solution: 1-2 weeks at 4°C, months at -20°C; 16% ampoules: 12 months sealed
incompatible_with: some epitopes are masked by PFA fixation (require antigen retrieval)
safety_notes: Releases formaldehyde (carcinogen); toxic by inhalation; use in fume hood; causes skin sensitization

name: Glutaraldehyde
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; EM-grade in sealed ampoules at 4°C; discard if yellow
preparation_notes: Stronger cross-linker than formaldehyde; preserves ultrastructure better. Causes high autofluorescence—NOT suitable for fluorescence microscopy unless quenched (NaBH4 or glycine). Often combined with PFA (2% PFA + 2.5% glutaraldehyde) for EM.
shelf_life: 6-12 months at -20°C; EM-grade ampoules: per manufacturer
incompatible_with: Tris buffers (reacts with primary amines; use phosphate or cacodylate buffer)
safety_notes: Toxic; severe irritant; potent sensitizer; use in fume hood; wear gloves

name: Triton X-100
storage_conditions: Room temperature; viscous liquid; cut pipette tip for accurate dispensing of neat solution
preparation_notes: Non-ionic detergent. CMC (critical micelle concentration) ~0.2 mM (~0.01%). Solubilizes membrane proteins while often preserving protein activity. Permeabilizes cell membranes for intracellular staining in immunofluorescence.
shelf_life: 24 months; old Triton X-100 may contain peroxides
safety_notes: Irritant; environmentally persistent (being phased out in EU; replaced by Triton X-100 reduced or other detergents)

name: Tween-20 (Polysorbate 20)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; viscous liquid; protect from light
preparation_notes: Mild non-ionic detergent. CMC ~0.06 mM (~0.007%). Does not typically lyse cells or denature proteins. Used as a blocking/washing agent to reduce non-specific binding in immunoassays.
shelf_life: 24 months; old Tween may contain peroxides
incompatible_with: some protein-protein interaction assays at high concentration
safety_notes: Very low toxicity; mild irritant
common_uses: TBST and PBST wash buffer component
reducing non-specific binding in western blots

name: NP-40 (Nonidet P-40) / IGEPAL CA-630
storage_conditions: Room temperature; note: original NP-40 is discontinued; IGEPAL CA-630 is the replacement with identical properties
preparation_notes: Non-ionic detergent similar to Triton X-100. Disrupts cell membranes but generally does not solubilize nuclear membranes at low concentrations (useful for cytoplasmic extracts). Component of RIPA buffer.
safety_notes: Irritant; similar safety profile to Triton X-100

name: LB Broth (Lysogeny Broth / Luria-Bertani)
working_concentration: 1x (10 g/L tryptone, 5 g/L yeast extract, 10 g/L NaCl)
storage_conditions: Autoclave (121°C, 15 psi, 15-20 minutes); store at room temperature; add antibiotics after cooling to <55°C
preparation_notes: The most common E. coli growth medium. Miller formulation (10 g/L NaCl) is standard. Lennox formulation has 5 g/L NaCl (better for salt-sensitive antibiotics like zeocin). Do NOT add glucose unless specifically needed (catabolite repression).
shelf_life: 3 months autoclaved at room temperature; check for contamination before use
safety_notes: Non-toxic; use sterile technique

name: LB Agar
storage_conditions: Store poured plates inverted at 4°C; pre-warm to 37°C before use
preparation_notes: Autoclave LB with agar. Cool to 55°C before adding antibiotics or supplements (higher temp destroys antibiotics). Pour ~25 mL per 100 mm plate. Store inverted to prevent condensation dripping on agar surface.
shelf_life: 1-2 months at 4°C (plates without antibiotics); 2-4 weeks with antibiotics
safety_notes: Non-toxic; use sterile technique; hot agar causes burns

name: SOC Medium
working_concentration: 1x (2% tryptone, 0.5% yeast extract, 10 mM NaCl, 2.5 mM KCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 10 mM MgSO4, 20 mM glucose)
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C in aliquots; or prepare fresh; filter-sterilize the glucose and Mg2+ separately and add after autoclaving
preparation_notes: SOC is SOB medium plus 20 mM glucose. Used for recovery after transformation to maximize transformation efficiency. Glucose and magnesium are critical; omitting them significantly reduces transformation efficiency. Pre-warm to 37°C before use.
safety_notes: Non-toxic; use sterile technique

name: DMEM (Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light; pre-warm to 37°C before use
preparation_notes: Contains high glucose (4.5 g/L) or low glucose (1 g/L). Supplement with L-glutamine (2 mM) or use GlutaMAX. Requires 5-10% CO2 incubator for bicarbonate buffering system. Phenol red pH indicator: red = pH 7.4; yellow = acidic; pink/purple = basic.
shelf_life: 12 months unopened; 4-6 weeks opened at 4°C; L-glutamine degrades over weeks
safety_notes: Non-toxic; use aseptic technique in biosafety cabinet
common_uses: culture of HEK293, HeLa, NIH/3T3, COS-7, and many adherent cell lines

name: RPMI-1640
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light; pre-warm to 37°C before use
preparation_notes: Originally developed for culture of human lymphoid cells. Contains biotin, vitamin B12, and PABA not found in DMEM. Lower glucose (2 g/L) than DMEM. Requires glutamine supplementation. Requires 5% CO2.
safety_notes: Non-toxic; use aseptic technique
common_uses: culture of suspension cells (Jurkat, K562, HL-60)

name: FBS (Fetal Bovine Serum)
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; thaw at 4°C overnight or in 37°C water bath; aliquot to avoid repeated freeze-thaw
preparation_notes: Heat-inactivate at 56°C for 30 min to destroy complement (required for some applications). Lot-to-lot variability is significant—test lots for your specific cell lines. Aliquot into 50 mL tubes. Maximum 3 freeze-thaw cycles.
shelf_life: 5 years at -20°C; 4-6 weeks after thawing at 4°C
safety_notes: Potential biohazard (animal-derived); handle with BSL-2 precautions; may contain endotoxins, mycoplasma, or other adventitious biological contaminants

name: Penicillin-Streptomycin (Pen-Strep)
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; thaw and aliquot; stable at 4°C in medium for 4 weeks
preparation_notes: Penicillin inhibits gram-positive bacteria (cell wall synthesis); streptomycin inhibits gram-negative bacteria (protein synthesis). Not a substitute for aseptic technique. May mask low-level contamination. Some cell types are sensitive to streptomycin.
shelf_life: 24 months at -20°C; 4 weeks in complete medium at 4°C
safety_notes: Penicillin may cause allergic reactions in sensitized individuals; avoid skin contact if penicillin-allergic
common_uses: routine cell culture bacterial contamination prevention

name: Trypsin-EDTA (0.05% or 0.25%)
working_concentration: 0.05% trypsin / 0.53 mM EDTA (standard); 0.25% for difficult-to-detach cells
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; working aliquots at 4°C for up to 2 weeks; pre-warm to 37°C before use
preparation_notes: EDTA chelates Ca2+/Mg2+ required for cadherin-mediated cell adhesion. Trypsin cleaves extracellular proteins. Neutralize with serum-containing medium (serum protease inhibitors inactivate trypsin). Over-trypsinization damages cell surface proteins and reduces viability.
incompatible_with: serum (inactivates trypsin; must wash cells with PBS before adding trypsin)
safety_notes: Protease; avoid skin contact; may cause irritation

name: PBS (Cell Culture Grade, Sterile)
working_concentration: 1x (Ca2+/Mg2+-free for cell dissociation; with Ca2+/Mg2+ for cell washing)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; sterile; endotoxin-tested for cell culture
preparation_notes: Ca2+/Mg2+-free PBS is used before trypsinization (divalent cations inhibit trypsin and maintain cell adhesion). PBS with Ca2+/Mg2+ is used when cell detachment is not desired. Must be sterile and endotoxin-free for cell culture.
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: Ampicillin
storage_conditions: Store powder at 4°C; stock solution at -20°C in aliquots; filter-sterilize stock
preparation_notes: Dissolve in water; filter-sterilize (0.22 µm); do NOT autoclave (heat-labile). Add to agar/media at <55°C. Ampicillin is bactericidal (kills growing cells) but degrades in media (beta-lactam ring hydrolysis). Plates lose effectiveness after 2-4 weeks. Satellite colonies appear if plates are old or inoculum is too dense.
shelf_life: Stock: 6 months at -20°C; plates: 2-4 weeks at 4°C
safety_notes: Potential allergen; avoid exposure if penicillin-allergic; beta-lactam antibiotic
common_uses: selection of bacteria carrying ampicillin-resistance plasmids (bla/AmpR)

name: Kanamycin
storage_conditions: Store powder at 4°C; stock solution at -20°C in aliquots; filter-sterilize stock
preparation_notes: Aminoglycoside antibiotic; more stable than ampicillin in media. Does not produce satellite colonies (a common problem with ampicillin). Can be autoclaved in solution (heat-stable), though filter-sterilization is preferred. Bactericidal.
shelf_life: Stock: 12 months at -20°C; plates: 4-8 weeks at 4°C
safety_notes: Potential ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity at therapeutic doses (not relevant at selection concentrations); handle with gloves
common_uses: selection of bacteria carrying kanamycin-resistance plasmids (kanR/nptII)

name: 1 kb DNA Ladder
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; stable at 4°C for months after thawing
preparation_notes: Load 0.5 µg per lane for ethidium bromide visualization. Typical bands: 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10 kb (varies by manufacturer). The brightest reference band (often 1 kb or 3 kb) is used for mass estimation. Mix gently; do not vortex.
safety_notes: Non-toxic
common_uses: DNA size estimation on agarose gels
approximate mass quantification of DNA bands

name: 100 bp DNA Ladder
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; stable at 4°C for months after thawing
preparation_notes: Bands typically at 100 bp intervals from 100 to 1000-1500 bp plus additional larger bands. Bright reference band at 500 or 600 bp (manufacturer-dependent). Use 1.5-2% agarose for best resolution of small fragments.
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: Pre-stained Protein Ladder (Protein Molecular Weight Marker)
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; do not boil (pre-stained markers are already denatured); aliquot to avoid freeze-thaw
preparation_notes: Load directly onto gel without heating. Pre-stained markers allow monitoring of electrophoresis and transfer. Molecular weights may differ slightly from actual MW due to dye binding. Typical range: 10-250 kDa. Some brands include color-coded bands at key MWs.
safety_notes: Non-toxic

name: Bradford Reagent (Coomassie G-250 Dye Reagent)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; protect from light; bring to room temperature before use
preparation_notes: Dye binds protein and shifts absorbance from 465 nm to 595 nm. Linear range: 1-25 µg/mL (microplate) or 100-1500 µg/mL (standard assay). Use BSA as standard. Read within 5-60 minutes. Compatible with most reducing agents and EDTA.
incompatible_with: high concentrations of Triton X-100 or NP-40
safety_notes: Contains phosphoric acid and methanol; mild irritant; avoid skin contact

name: BCA Protein Assay Reagent
working_concentration: Working reagent: 50:1 ratio Reagent A:Reagent B
stock_concentration: Reagent A (bicinchoninic acid) and Reagent B (4% CuSO4·5H2O)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; Reagent B at room temperature; working reagent should be prepared fresh
preparation_notes: Cu2+ reduced to Cu+ by protein in alkaline conditions; Cu+ chelated by BCA producing purple color (562 nm). Linear range: 20-2000 µg/mL (standard), 0.5-20 µg/mL (micro). Incubate 30 min at 37°C. More tolerant of detergents than Bradford but affected by reducing agents.
incompatible_with: reducing agents (DTT, BME) cause high background
safety_notes: Contains sodium hydroxide and copper sulfate; mild irritant
common_uses: protein quantification compatible with detergent-containing samples

name: Lipofectamine (Lipofectamine 2000/3000)
working_concentration: per manufacturer protocol (typically 2-5 µL per well in 6-well plate)
storage_conditions: Store at 2-8°C; do NOT freeze; gently mix before use; do NOT vortex
preparation_notes: Cationic lipid-based transfection reagent. Dilute in Opti-MEM (serum-free medium), NOT in complete medium with serum. DNA:lipofectamine ratio must be optimized for each cell line. Remove after 4-6 hours and replace with complete medium. Lipofectamine 3000 includes P3000 enhancer reagent.
incompatible_with: serum in complexing step (serum inhibits lipoplex formation)
safety_notes: Cationic lipid; cytotoxic at high concentrations; minimize exposure time

name: Polyethyleneimine (PEI, Linear 25 kDa)
working_concentration: 1-3 µg PEI per µg DNA (typical N/P ratio 10-20)
storage_conditions: Store stock at -20°C or -80°C; filter-sterilize; aliquot to avoid freeze-thaw
preparation_notes: Dissolve in water, adjust pH to 7.0 with HCl, filter-sterilize (0.22 µm). Linear PEI (25 kDa) is preferred over branched for transfection. Mix PEI and DNA in serum-free medium, incubate 15-20 min to form polyplexes. Much cheaper alternative to Lipofectamine.
safety_notes: Irritant; cytotoxic at high concentrations; handle with gloves
common_uses: large-scale transient transfection of reporter plasmids

name: Poly-L-Lysine
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C or -20°C; sterile
preparation_notes: Coat surface for 1 hour at 37°C or overnight at 4°C. Aspirate and rinse 2-3x with sterile water (not PBS—salt interferes with coating). Air dry before seeding cells. MW 70-150 kDa most common for cell culture. Enhances cell attachment through electrostatic interaction.
safety_notes: Non-toxic at coating concentrations
common_uses: enhancing cell attachment for neurons and other poorly adherent cells

name: Collagen (Type I, Rat Tail)
storage_conditions: Store at 4°C; do NOT freeze (denatures collagen); keep sterile; acidic solution
preparation_notes: Dilute in 0.02 M acetic acid or cold PBS. Coat at room temperature for 1-2 hours or 4°C overnight. Rinse with PBS before seeding cells. Collagen gels at neutral pH and 37°C (used for 3D culture). Keep solutions cold to prevent gelation during handling.
safety_notes: Animal-derived; handle with BSL-1 precautions; non-toxic

name: Fibronectin
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; thaw at 4°C; aliquot to avoid freeze-thaw; do not vortex
preparation_notes: Dilute in PBS or serum-free medium. Coat at 37°C for 1 hour or 4°C overnight. Fibronectin adsorbs to plastic surfaces from solution. Works synergistically with collagen for some cell types.
safety_notes: Animal or human-derived; handle appropriately; non-toxic

name: PMSF (Phenylmethylsulfonyl Fluoride)
stock_concentration: 100 mM or 200 mM in isopropanol or DMSO
storage_conditions: Store stock at -20°C; dissolve in isopropanol or DMSO (insoluble in water); add fresh to buffers immediately before use
preparation_notes: Irreversible serine protease inhibitor. Half-life in aqueous solution is ~35 min at pH 8.0 and 25°C—must add fresh immediately before use. Insoluble in water; dissolve in isopropanol or DMSO first. Inhibits trypsin, chymotrypsin, and thrombin.
shelf_life: Stock: 12 months at -20°C; active in aqueous solution for ~30-60 minutes only
incompatible_with: aqueous solutions (rapidly hydrolyzes; add immediately before use)
safety_notes: Toxic; cholinesterase inhibitor; extremely destructive to mucous membranes; wear gloves and eye protection
common_uses: serine protease inhibition during protein extraction

name: Proteinase K
stock_concentration: 20 mg/mL in water or storage buffer
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; working solution at 4°C for short-term; self-digestion may occur over time
preparation_notes: Broad-spectrum serine protease active in SDS, urea, EDTA. Active over wide pH range (4-12) with optimum at pH 7.5-8.0. Active at 37-65°C. Inactivate by heating to 95°C for 10 min or by adding PMSF (5 mM). Degrades nucleases during DNA/RNA extraction.
safety_notes: Protease; avoid skin contact and inhalation; wear gloves
common_uses: removal of nucleases from DNA/RNA preparations

name: RNase A
stock_concentration: 10 mg/mL in 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 15 mM NaCl
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; extremely stable enzyme; heat to 100°C for 15 min to eliminate DNase contamination
preparation_notes: Heat stock solution to 100°C for 15 min to inactivate contaminating DNases. Active over wide pH range. Requires no cofactors. Cleaves ssRNA after pyrimidine residues. Very stable enzyme; difficult to fully inactivate (resists autoclaving and many denaturants).
safety_notes: Non-toxic; extreme caution to avoid contaminating RNA experiments—trace amounts destroy RNA
common_uses: removal of RNA from DNA preparations (plasmid minipreps)
flow cytometry cell cycle analysis (with PI)

name: DNase I (RNase-free)
storage_conditions: Store at -20°C; requires divalent cations (Mg2+ or Mn2+) for activity; aliquot to avoid freeze-thaw
preparation_notes: With Mg2+: nicks dsDNA randomly. With Mn2+: cleaves both strands at approximately the same site (blunt-end cuts). Inactivate with EDTA (chelates required Mg2+) and/or heat (75°C, 10 min with EDTA). RNase-free grade essential for RNA applications.
safety_notes: Non-toxic; wear gloves to avoid nuclease contamination
common_uses: removal of DNA from RNA preparations
on-column DNase treatment during RNA extraction

name: Sodium Hydroxide (NaOH)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; store in polyethylene/polypropylene (dissolves glass over time); absorbs CO2 from air reducing concentration
preparation_notes: Highly exothermic when dissolving; add slowly to water. Used for alkaline lysis in plasmid minipreps (0.2 M NaOH + 1% SDS). Denatures dsDNA. Store in plastic bottles (NaOH etches glass). Titrate fresh for precise pH work.
safety_notes: Corrosive; causes severe burns; wear gloves and eye protection; immediate water rinse for skin contact

name: Hydrochloric Acid (HCl)
storage_conditions: Room temperature; tightly sealed; fume hood for concentrated solutions
preparation_notes: Always add acid to water, not water to acid. Used for pH adjustment of Tris buffers. Concentrated HCl fumes in air.
safety_notes: Corrosive; concentrated solution fumes toxic HCl gas; use in fume hood; wear gloves and eye protection
common_uses: pH adjustment of Tris and other buffers

name: Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA)
working_concentration: 1-5% for blocking; 0.1-1 mg/mL as enzyme stabilizer
storage_conditions: Powder at 4°C; solutions at -20°C in aliquots; filter-sterilize for cell culture
preparation_notes: Dissolve gently (do not vortex—causes foaming and denaturation). Use BSA (not milk) for blocking with phospho-specific antibodies or biotin-streptavidin systems. Fraction V is standard lab grade. Fatty acid-free BSA for lipid-sensitive assays.
safety_notes: Non-toxic; animal-derived; wear gloves

name: Sodium Deoxycholate
storage_conditions: Room temperature; protect from light; hygroscopic
preparation_notes: Anionic detergent; bile salt derivative. Component of RIPA buffer. Solubilizes membrane proteins. Precipitates in acidic solutions (pH <6.0). Not compatible with ion-exchange chromatography.
incompatible_with: divalent cations at high concentrations (forms insoluble salts)
safety_notes: Irritant; avoid inhalation of powder
